title: README: Soften messaging without losing technical honesty
description:
## Problem

Current README says:

> "Meshtastic, MeshCore, and LoRaWAN are neat. They're also deeply flawed."

"Deeply flawed" is a value judgment that will trigger defensive reactions from Meshtastic community members. The technical criticisms in the table are accurate and sufficient — no need to editorialize.

## Change

Replace:
> Meshtastic, MeshCore, and LoRaWAN are neat. They're also deeply flawed.

With:
> Meshtastic, MeshCore, and LoRaWAN are useful. They also hit limits that matter for some use cases.

## Rationale

- "Useful" acknowledges real value (people use them successfully)
- "Hit limits" is factual, not judgmental
- "For some use cases" implies LICHEN is for different use cases, not universally better
- The table still does the work of explaining the technical differences
- Engineers will read the table; the intro just sets tone

## Why this matters

The Meshtastic community includes:
- True believers who will be hostile regardless (ignore them)
- Technical people who respect honest comparison (keep the table)
- Casual users considering switching (don't insult their current choice)

The goal is to make it easy for group 3 to try LICHEN without feeling they have to defend Meshtastic. "Flawed" makes them defensive. "Hits limits" makes them curious.

## Additional softening (optional)

Could also change the table header from "Problem" to "Limitation" or "Constraint" — same information, less charged language.

| Constraint | Meshtastic | MeshCore | LoRaWAN |

This is minor but consistent with the tone shift.
close_reason: Updated README.md to use softer neutral language per issue. Changed 'deeply flawed' to 'hit limits that matter for some use cases' and table header to 'Limitation'. Maintains technical accuracy while improving community tone.
